best_edges.evidence: pullback_long produced $1129.1 over 14 trades with 417.6 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE only -138.25, median MFE 102.1, and median hold 11.1 minutes. This is the cleanest scalable active edge in the current run.

charm_pin_pullback_long is top PnL at $1526.2 over 39 trades, median hold 6.7 minutes, and average MAE -84.1. It carries the worst -527 MAE, but broad pruning would remove 24.8% of total PnL and many fast winners.

best_edges.action: protect but avoid broad quality tightening
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte generated $1304 over 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades with 100% win rate. Prior 1DTE local quality gates cut score by -355 to -594 and worsened MAE/drawdown, so the edge is real but sensitive to over-filtering.

best_edges.action: under-test only with small isolated samples
best_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long produced $973.1 over only 5 trades, 234.7 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, and 12-point median TP. Sample is too small for aggressive changes, but reward per trade is high.

call_wall_acceptance_long has only 1 trade, but it showed $91 PnL, 455 PnL/exposure hour, -11.7 MAE, setup quality 72.5, and ADX 42.8. Prior call-wall loosening hurt score, so expansion must be isolated and tiny.

broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long produced zero trades while currently disabled or unreachable. There is no current-run evidence to optimize them for profit expansion.
broken_edges.problem: dead or inactive active-router profiles with no realized sample

broken_edges.evidence: Two historical 1DTE quality-gate tests reduced score by -355.7 and -594.3, cut net profit by -317.1 to -558.5, and worsened worst MAE to -603.75.
broken_edges.problem: quality filters removed profitable insurance without controlling risk

broken_edges.evidence: Fallback-prune-pullback-retain was flat to negative: score -21.4, net profit -23.4, exposure higher, and worst MAE unchanged. Latest stale sample is only 5 trades and still profitable at $162.8.
broken_edges.problem: small stale/no-0DTE sample does not justify more pruning

broken_edges.action: avoid blunt extended-VWAP or structure caps
broken_edges.evidence: Bad-MAE rows overlap with protected extended-VWAP winners: 18 clean extended-VWAP winners contributed $674.9, while entry-candidate caps such as rolling-high distance or ADX slope would block many protected good trades and retain only 58.6%-69.8% PnL in counterfactuals.
broken_edges.problem: bad-tail features are not cleanly separable with broad entry filters

broken_edges.action: do not use runtime health exits for profit expansion
broken_edges.evidence: The runtime-risk validation improved worst MAE but cut score by -4668.6 and net profit by -544.2. For a no-stop strategy with all winners, this converts recoverable heat into objective damage.
broken_edges.problem: post-entry risk controls suppress objective too heavily

market_regime_read: The current sample is a favorable long-only, take-profit-only regime with 96 completed trades, 100% realized win rate, no >=12h slow trades, 0.4% drawdown, and $6148.6 PnL. The leader baseline remains run_001/run_005/run_014 at $7125.8, 107 trades, 36.17 exposure hours, and 196.98 PnL/exposure hour. Latest run_015 has lower total PnL but slightly higher PnL/exposure hour at 200.72 and lower average MAE, meaning the latest changes improved selectivity but starved profitable throughput. Profit-expansion should therefore restore or add selective throughput around proven active edges, not add broad risk filters. Watch MAE because the entire system still has a hidden no-stop tail: worst MAE remains -527 and many trades reach MAE before meaningful MFE.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: 0DTE-unavailable entries may include pullback/reclaim states that are not true breakouts.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Could cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades if router ordering or profile matching is too broad.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries split by ES pullback/reclaim structure, VWAP distance, RSI bucket, and option-map quality.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current breakout_long_1dte PnL, 1DTE average MAE better than -123.7, total PnL >= 98% of leader, and no worse worst MAE.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: High-extension charm-pin entries appear to contain both fast clean scalps and rare deep-heat recoveries.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: A code-level split may overfit entry-path features or duplicate existing vetoes without improving out-of-sample behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Profile-scoped features for VWAP extension, pullback-from-high, rolling-high recency, RSI slope, first observed move, and early MFE/MAE path state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long entries with high VWAP extension and slow-to-profit/deep-heat fingerprints.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -375 while retaining >=90% of charm-pin PnL and preserving fast winner count.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Call-wall acceptance had one very clean high-efficiency trade, but prior broad loosening failed.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Relaxed flow gates can add noisy wall-chase entries or steal signals from stronger pullback profiles.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Micro-Sample Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Accepted call-wall entries with net DEX, forward DEX, call volume acceleration, wall distance, and ES structure strength.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE accepted call-wall context with strong ADX/structure and positive call-flow expansion.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5 trades, positive PnL, PnL/exposure hour >250, average MAE better than -60, and no total-score regression beyond 2%.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant pullback_long_1dte is the only dormant one-DTE concept explicitly allowed and may complement breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router activation may be unreachable or may divert high-quality breakout 1DTE trades into a weaker profile.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant pullback_long_1dte Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY routing, 1DTE option-map state, ES pullback/reclaim fields, and profile-level PnL/MAE attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable with ES pullback/reclaim structure and valid 1DTE option map.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: New profile adds or reallocates 1DTE trades with combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1304, better average MAE, and total score not worse than -2%.

next_experiments.config_bias: Profile-specific profit expansion: loosen or restore only the least risky pullback_long entry boundaries around structure/VWAP distance that were tightened in the latest lower-PnL run. Avoid global filters and preserve current MAE guardrails.
next_experiments.goal: Recover profitable throughput lost versus the leader while preserving run_015's stronger PnL/exposure hour.
next_experiments.hypothesis: pullback_long has enough efficiency and low worst MAE that small throughput expansion can lift total PnL without adding the system tail.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Throughput Re-Expansion
next_experiments.primary_metric: Total score and net PnL versus leader, with pullback_long PnL/exposure hour >=300 and worst MAE not worse than -200.
next_experiments.risk: The latest tightening may have removed lower-quality pullbacks; restoring too much could reintroduce deep heat from earlier runs.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not prune charm_pin_pullback_long broadly. Test only a narrow profit-expansion variant that preserves high-extension fast winners while slightly improving exits/TP opportunity where MFE supports it. Avoid setup-quality floors and broad VWAP caps.
next_experiments.goal: Increase charm-pin PnL per trade without materially increasing exposure hours or blocking fast winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The charm-pin edge is high-frequency and profitable, but current TP behavior leaves MFE unused in some winners; profile-scoped TP expansion may improve PnL more safely than entry pruning.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin TP Harvest Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long PnL, PnL/exposure hour, median hold, and average MAE; reject if worst MAE worsens or fast_under_15m trades fall by more than 3.
next_experiments.risk: Larger TP targets can turn quick scalps into longer heat cycles in a no-stop system.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate breakout_long_1dte profit expansion from quality tightening. Keep the profile active and avoid raising structure/slope floors. Explore only modest reward/throughput improvements that do not reduce trade count by more than 1-2.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE PnL/exposure hour while retaining the unavailable-0DTE insurance role.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Prior 1DTE filters failed because they reduced profitable sample size; the better test is to preserve entries and tune profit capture or very mild routing rather than gate quality.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Preserve-And-Harvest
next_experiments.primary_metric: breakout_long_1dte PnL >= current $1304, PnL/exposure hour >180, average MAE no worse than -125, total PnL >=98% of leader.
next_experiments.risk: All 1DTE trades are winners in-sample, so any sample reduction can look risk-aware while damaging real objective.

next_experiments.config_bias: Small sample-builder only: activate or lightly expand call_wall_acceptance_long without changing global option-call-volume thresholds that previously hurt score. Keep any experiment isolated to the profile's own acceptance/TP behavior.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether call-wall acceptance can become a real active edge rather than a one-trade artifact.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The lone call-wall trade was very clean, but broad loosening failed; a profile-local sample builder may add high-quality wall trades without damaging other profiles.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Local Sample Builder
next_experiments.primary_metric: At least 3-5 call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive profile PnL, average MAE better than -60, and total score drawdown less than 2%.
next_experiments.risk: Single-trade evidence is fragile and profile may steal stronger pullback/squeeze signals.

next_experiments.config_bias: Router experiment only if legal as one logical dormant activation: add pullback_long_1dte to the one-DTE router alongside breakout_long_1dte and tune only that same dormant profile. Do not change existing breakout_long_1dte gates in the same test.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether 1DTE pullback state improves the current 1DTE fallback bucket.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY is profitable but tail-heavy; a dedicated pullback companion may improve average MAE or PnL/exposure without suppressing breakout insurance.
next_experiments.name: Dormant 1DTE Pullback Activation Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined VALID_1DTE_ONLY PnL, average MAE, worst MAE, trade count, and total score versus leader.
next_experiments.risk: Router ordering may divert profitable breakout trades or the dormant profile may be unreachable.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep fallback profiles unchanged unless the optimizer runs a dedicated stale-data holdout. Do not mix fallback pruning with main profit-expansion tests.
next_experiments.goal: Prevent small-sample stale/no-0DTE edits from obscuring main-edge profit-expansion results.
next_experiments.hypothesis: No-0DTE fallback evidence is too sparse and prior pruning was not beneficial; preserving it improves experiment interpretability.
next_experiments.name: Fallback Freeze Control
next_experiments.primary_metric: No unintended change in STALE_0DTE/EXPIRED_0DTE trade attribution while primary profile experiments run.
next_experiments.risk: A rare stale fallback tail may remain, but the current sample is profitable and too small for reliable optimization.

research_notes: The main strategic read is that run_015 improved selectivity but gave up too much leader throughput: 96 trades and $6148.6 versus leader 107 trades and $7125.8. Since objective mode is risk-adjusted and latest PnL/exposure hour is already slightly above leader, the best profit-expansion path is not broad loosening; it is profile-local restoration around the cleanest edges. Avoid repeating failed families: 1DTE quality gates, call-wall global loosening, fallback pruning, and runtime health exits all hurt prior objectives. The bad-MAE analysis argues against blunt entry filters because tail signatures overlap protected fast and extended-VWAP winners. If the optimizer wants a risk-aware companion to profit expansion, prefer narrow profile-specific tests and evaluate retained PnL before celebrating MAE improvement.
risk_controls: Reject any profit-expansion test if total net PnL falls below 98% of the leader without a compensating PnL/exposure-hour gain.
Reject if worst MAE worsens beyond -527 or average MAE deteriorates materially versus run_015.
Reject if completed trades drop more than 5% in a profit-expansion experiment unless PnL/exposure hour rises enough to offset total PnL loss.
Track fast_under_15m trades; losing more than 3-5 fast winners is a warning that the experiment is filtering the core edge.
Keep fallback/no-0DTE edits isolated from main profile experiments because stale samples are tiny.
Do not optimize inactive zero-trade profiles until they are part of an explicit activation experiment.
